meta.description: Pilot jurisdiction scoring matrix. Each candidate jurisdiction is scored on criteria relevant to data center zoning intelligence. Scores are 0 (unavailable) to 3 (excellent).

criteria.zoning_geometry_available: Official zoning polygon GIS data is publicly available (0=no, 1=PDF map only, 2=MapServer, 3=FeatureServer/download)
criteria.parcel_data_available: Official parcel GIS data is publicly available (0=no, 1=limited, 2=MapServer, 3=FeatureServer/download)
criteria.ordinance_available: Zoning ordinance is publicly accessible online (0=no, 1=PDF only old, 2=PDF current, 3=searchable online)
criteria.dimensional_table_available: Dimensional standards are in a structured table (0=no, 1=buried in text, 2=PDF table, 3=clear online table)
criteria.use_table_available: Permitted use table is available (0=no, 1=scattered in text, 2=PDF table, 3=clear online table)
criteria.data_center_relevance: Relevance to data center policy and planning (0=none, 1=low, 2=medium, 3=critical)
criteria.update_feasibility: How easy it is to automatically check for updates (0=none, 1=hard, 2=some automation, 3=full automation via ArcGIS)
criteria.licensing_clarity: How clear the data use terms are (0=unknown, 1=unclear, 2=public but unclear terms, 3=explicit open license)

jurisdictions.notes: World's largest data center market. Exceptional GIS infrastructure. Municode ordinance. Data Center Overlay District exists. PD-IP zone is the gold standard for data center zoning. SELECTED AS PILOT 1.
Adjacent to Loudoun. Major data center market. Excellent GIS (ArcGIS). Active zoning enforcement on data centers. Has I-5 and I-6 industrial districts where data centers operate.
Rapidly growing data center market extending from Loudoun. County had significant data center moratorium debate in 2023-2024. Good ArcGIS infrastructure.
Excellent open data. Growing data center market. Maryland's most advanced county GIS program. Clear licensing via Montgomery County open data portal.

Excellent open data (open.dc.gov). DC Zoning Handbook is highly structured. DC ZR16 has clear use categories. However, DC data center presence is primarily carrier hotels in downtown. Phase 3 given regulatory complexity of DC government.
Research Triangle data center hub (Raleigh/Apex/Morrisville). Apple, Google, Meta all have facilities nearby. Wake County has good GIS but ordinance navigation is more complex.

Chatham County has experienced significant controversy over data center development including the Apple campus. More limited GIS resources than Wake. Include for policy context rather than GIS completeness.
